title: Native image I/O — Linux via libpng + libjpeg-turbo + libwebp
goal: Implement the Linux backend for the image-I/O dispatch table defined in ESH-0006. Uses system libpng (PNG), libjpeg-turbo (JPEG/BMP), and libwebp (WebP). Auto-detected via pkg-config. Native to the platform.
next_actions: lib/core/image_io_linux.c: implement the three function-pointer slots using libpng (png_read_image / png_write_image), libjpeg-turbo (jpeg_read_scanlines / jpeg_write_scanlines), and libwebp (WebPDecodeRGBA / WebPEncodeRGBA).
Use libswscale or a simple bilinear resampler for resize (no GLU/freeimage dependency — keep the dependency surface minimal).
Wire into CMakeLists.txt: LINUX branch pkg_check_modules(PNG libpng) + pkg_check_modules(JPEG libjpeg) + pkg_check_modules(WEBP libwebp). Link the three libs.
Add tests/v1_3_edge_cases/image_io_linux_test.esk: same shape as the Darwin test (read PNG, round-trip JPEG, resize verification).
acceptance: lib/core/image_io_linux.c provides read/write/resize matching the contract.
Linux build links libpng + libjpeg + libwebp via pkg-config; no deps/stb.
image_io_linux_test passes on cosbox AND old-donkey (x86_64) AND xavier (aarch64).
verification: pkg-config --exists libpng && pkg-config --exists libjpeg && pkg-config --exists libwebp
